angle: Recursion / community (you backed companies here, now the platform opens up)
hook_type: Personal address + cadence numbers + recursion in first sentence
tactic: 'You did this' personal-address proof -> the platform you used is now raising -> waitlist
primary_text:
137,000 of you backed 324 companies on our platform. Now the platform is raising.

Every one of those raises, $234M+ in total, came from people choosing to own a piece of something they believed in, not from VCs. You built that. Now we are opening our own Crowd-Sourced Funding raise on it.

The waitlist is free. You see the offer document before you decide anything.

Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: You built this. Now we are raising.
description: Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live.
_source_label: DRC V5 -- W3 Recursion / Community (existing investors)
_iterates: BIRCHAL-V4-B1 (V4 quality bar). Recursion stated with both referents explicit per learnings-meta-ad.md item 1/3.
_v5_rationale: Carries the proven recursion-as-community spine the brief flags as strongest (fuses ownership + belonging + proof). 'You backed 324 companies ... now the platform is raising ... You built that. Now we are opening our own raise on it' makes both referents explicit (you = the investor; we/the platform = the company raising). No ambiguous 'it'/'itself' (learnings anti-pattern).
Hook Halbert 5/5 in --primary-text mode: personal address + 137,000 + 324 cadence + recursion.
Names it a 'Crowd-Sourced Funding raise' once so Segment B is clear this is the live-offer event, addressing the client's 'doesn't read like a raise' note in the most-aware cluster too.
_drc_playbook: 11 Lifecycle Email Promo / Launch (B2C considered, most-aware, active list)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who have made at least one investment on the platform.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'you will see the offer'. No transaction verbs. 'backed' is past-tense factual about prior raises.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'you will see the offer the moment it goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
Anti-preferential terms (s 738G): waitlist remains free and equal-access, no preferential framing vs the public offer.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' appears zero times in body; 'our platform' / first-person we/our throughout; recursion subjects unambiguous (you = investor, we/platform = company raising).
